sanskrit:
शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म प्रारभते नरः।
न्याय्यं वा विपरीतं वा पञ्चैतस्य हेतवः॥
transliteration: śarīra-vāṅ-manobhir yat karma prārabhate naraḥ | nya̅yyaṃ vā viparītaṃ vā pañcaite tasya hetavaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Whatever action a person initiates by body, speech, or mind—whether proper or improper—has these five as its causes.
contextual_meaning: Every deed, good or bad, is not a solo performance but the product of five interacting factors: body, senses, life-breath, mind, and the deeper field of latent tendencies. Recognizing this diffuses the illusion of isolated authorship and invites responsible engagement rather than shame or grandiosity.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance between felt personal responsibility and the distributed nature of action; opens space for integrated self-compassion and ethical accountability.
trigger_scenario: After snapping at your child, you spiral into 'I'm a terrible parent' instead of seeing the convergence of fatigue, stress hormones, sensory overload, and old scripts—realizing all can be adjusted.
anchor_text: See every act as fivefold, then act with steady, humble care.
